reasoning: The Lean statement correctly formalizes the informal claim. Polynomials are over ℚ matching "rational coefficients". The hypothesis `∀ n, ∃ a : ℤ, (f*g).coeff n = a` (with implicit coercion ℤ → ℚ) is the standard Mathlib idiom for "every coefficient of f·g is an integer", matching "f(x)g(x) has integer coefficients". The free variables i, j : ℕ are universally quantified at the theorem level, correctly capturing "any coefficient ... any coefficient". The conclusion `∃ a : ℤ, f.coeff i * g.coeff j = a` likewise says the product is an integer. The hypothesis and conclusion are logically equivalent to the informal statement — no hypotheses are dropped, added, or altered.
